news.title: Ashiana Housing buys 28.55 acre land in Pune to build senior living homes
news.para: Ashiana Housing has acquired 28.55 acres of land in Pune for its largest senior living project to date. The development is expected to generate an estimated revenue of Rs 1,800 crore, catering to significant demand for senior homes in the region. This strategic acquisition follows a period of strong sales growth for the company.

news.title: Vedanta questions evaluation metrics behind selection of Adani's bid for JAL
news.para: Vedanta Group questioned lenders' evaluation metrics for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL), alleging their chosen bid from Adani Enterprises was lower. Vedanta argued their bid offered higher gross and net present value, and the evaluation process did not prioritize value maximization as per insolvency framework objectives.

news.title: Mumbai civic body MCGM to deploy AI system to overhaul real estate approvals
news.para: Mumbai's Municipal Corporation is set to deploy an advanced AI-driven system to streamline real estate approvals, aiming to expedite project clearances and enhance transparency. This initiative will integrate multiple civic processes onto a unified digital platform, reducing manual intervention and improving data-driven decision-making for developers and authorities.

news.title: Chandigarh administration set to notify bed and breakfast policy
news.para: Chandigarh is set to formalize its booming Bed & Breakfast sector with a new policy, aiming to prevent the conversion of homes into mini-hotels. The 'Bed & Breakfast Policy 2026' will enforce strict regulations, ensuring B&Bs maintain a residential appearance and avoid commercial activities beyond guest services.

news.title: Jaiprakash Gaur backs Adani Group's acquisition of JAL assets
news.para: Jaypee Group founder Jaiprakash Gaur has expressed full confidence in the Adani Group's leadership to take over his insolvent firm, JAL. This statement follows the Committee of Creditors' selection of Adani Enterprises' bid, a decision Gaur respects despite a challenge from rival bidder Vedanta. Gaur believes Adani will carry forward JAL's legacy with renewed energy and responsibility.

news.title: SC orders Nowhera to surrender in a week, links bail to sale deeds execution
news.para: The Supreme Court has ordered Heera Group chief Nowhera Shaik to surrender within a week, warning of a non-bailable warrant if she fails to comply. Her bail will depend on her cooperation in executing 16 pending sale deeds for auction purchasers, a process crucial for resolving a Rs 10,000 crore fraud involving thousands of investors.

news.title: Nagpur civic body plans offer up to 15% rebate as 44% property owners avail tax sops
news.para: The Nagpur Municipal Corporation (NMC) plans to reintroduce property tax rebates of up to 15% following last year's success, where nearly 44% of property holders availed the incentive. The proposed structure aims to maximize early collections and promote digital payments, offering 15% and 10% rebates for payments by June 30, 2026.

news.title: Ajmera Realty reports pre-sales of ₹1,701 crore in FY26
news.para: Ajmera Realty & Infra India Ltd reported a significant 57% year-on-year increase in pre-sales, reaching ₹1,701 crore in FY2026. This growth was driven by new project launches and strong market demand, with collections also rising by 71% to ₹1,103 crore. The company achieved its highest-ever pre-sales and record collections, demonstrating exceptional market absorption.

news.title: Lucknow development body to offer over 2,000 plots in first phase of new housing scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is set to launch the Varun Vihar housing scheme in June, offering approximately 2,100 plots in its initial phase. Developed over 2,270 hectares along the Agra expressway, the scheme's land acquisition is progressing through mutual consent. Online registrations will commence post-launch.

news.title: Mysuru development body earns ₹156 crore as buyers lap up premium sites
news.para: Mysuru Development Authority (MDA) generated Rs 156 crore from auctioning 133 out of 200 commercial and residential sites. Buyers showed strong interest across Mysuru, particularly in West Mysuru where sites fetched Rs 13,000 per sqft. Vasanth Nagar also saw significant interest, with 33 of 40 sites sold.

news.title: Cyberabad civic body commissioner asks builders to implement safety measures at construction sites
news.para: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation Commissioner G Srijana has directed builders to strictly follow construction norms, environmental standards, and safety regulations. Developers must implement dust control measures and manage construction waste responsibly. Partial Occupancy Certificates will only be issued for projects built according to approved plans, with no deviations in buffer zones.

news.title: Godrej Properties reports bookings of ₹34,171 crore in FY26
news.para: Godrej Properties achieved its highest-ever annual bookings of ₹34,171 crore in FY2026. This marks a significant 16% growth year-on-year. The company sold 17,515 units, with total sales volume rising 5%. Collections also saw a healthy increase. New business development additions promise a strong future launch pipeline. Promoters increased their stake in the company.

news.title: New Noida land rates at par with YEIDA at ₹4,300 per sq meters
news.para: Noida Authority has set land acquisition rates for the upcoming phases of New Noida. The Dadri-Noida-Ghaziabad Investment Region project will span 209 sq km. Land rates for the third and fourth phases are fixed at Rs 4,300 per sqm. This move aims to expedite the development of a modern industrial hub.

news.title: Bombay HC directs MMRDA to refund ₹646 crore to Reliance Industries
news.para: Bombay High Court has ordered the MMRDA to refund Rs 646 crore to Reliance Industries Limited, setting aside penalties for an alleged delay in completing a convention centre. The court deemed the penalty levy arbitrary and unreasonable, stating MMRDA was obligated to apply a six-year extension clause to the project.

news.title: OC probe widens as checks reveal lapses, 14 of 22 buildings incomplete in Gurugram
news.para: An ongoing probe into occupation certificate irregularities in Gurugram has uncovered widespread violations. A joint team inspected 22 buildings in Sector 70A, finding at least 14 incomplete structures were granted OCs. Architects involved in issuing these certificates to incomplete buildings are facing scrutiny, with strict action, including license suspension, being considered.

news.title: Gujarat RERA imposes ₹3.5 crore penalties on builders for violating bank account norms
news.para: GujRERA has fined developers Rs 3.5 crore for not following rules on RERA bank accounts. This action targets builders who failed to deposit homebuyer funds correctly. The regulator is strictly overseeing project funds. Fines range from Rs 95,000 to Rs 2 crore. This move signals a tougher stance on financial discipline in real estate.

news.title: Bombay HC upholds Altamount Road building redevelopment, dismisses 2013 PIL
news.para: The Bombay High Court dismissed a 2013 public interest litigation challenging permissions for a 16-storey redevelopment on Altamount Road. The court found no genuine public interest, stating the PIL aimed to stall the builder. It upheld the permissions, noting adherence to development control regulations and deeming the petition misleading.

news.title: ED probes purported financial links of Kolkata-based Merlin Group
news.para: Enforcement Directorate officials are investigating alleged financial links between a Kolkata-based realty company and West Bengal politicians and officials. Raids were conducted at seven premises linked to the Merlin Group. Investigators seized digital devices and documents as part of a money laundering probe. The company stated it is cooperating with the authorities.

news.title: Whitestone REIT to be taken private by Ares in $1.7 billion deal
news.para: Ares Management will buy Whitestone REIT in a $1.7 billion all-cash deal.  Whitestone shareholders will receive $19 per share.  This acquisition follows months of takeover interest and shareholder pressure.  The deal is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026.  The transaction has received unanimous board approval.
